mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate (0.16% as of Dec 2012) lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and signals continued central bank accommodation, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of trailing 12-month windows while FEDFUNDS stays flat near zero, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points over two consecutive months signals accelerating labor-market healing, which historically precedes positive equity performance over the subsequent quarter as consumption and earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by >=0.2pp over 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If triggered instances show a positive forward 60-day SPX return rate below 55% or an average Sharpe ratio below zero across the sample, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and construction demand expansion, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month beyond what either series predicts individually.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM change > 0.5% AND HOUST MoM change > 2%
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger sample's mean 20-day forward return is not significantly greater (t-test p>0.10) than the return following either single-series trigger alone, reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: A steadily rising industrial production index during the early-cycle recovery confirms supply-side strength consistent with the ongoing post-crisis expansion, predicting continued equity gains over the next quarter as fundamentals validate market pricing.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery following the 2008-09 financial crisis
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO's 6-month trend and forward 60-day SPX returns turns negative or falls below |r|=0.05 during the recovery regime, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in headline CPI year-over-year inflation above 2.5% raises expectations of future monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets reprice rate-hike risk.
failure_condition: If triggered CPI-threshold occurrences produce non-negative average 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in monthly nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals rising labor income and consumer spending capacity, which positively predicts S&P 500 returns over the next month via improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and forward 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 across the full 2005-2024 sample, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2% annualized, confirmed by concurrent retail sales growth, signals synchronized broad-based demand expansion that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter beyond the signal from GDP alone, mitigating GDP's high revision risk.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: If joint-trigger periods fail to outperform GDPC1-only-trigger periods by at least 1 percentage point in average forward 60-day return, reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding -0.5% signals an abrupt supply-side shock that triggers immediate risk-off equity selling over the following week as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: If triggered INDPRO contraction events show positive SPX 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, reject.
